---
title: "InferenceController"
---

## Overview

**Header:** `DeepLearning_Widget/Inference/InferenceController.hpp`  
**Source:** `src/WhiskerToolbox/DeepLearning_Widget/Inference/`

`InferenceController` orchestrates deep learning inference runs (single frame,
batch, multi-interval batch, and recurrent sequence). It owns the background
worker thread lifecycle and delegates result merging to `ResultProcessor`.

The properties widget delegates run actions to this controller and forwards
progress signals to the view.

## Public API

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `runSingleFrame(frame)` | Encode and decode one frame on the GUI thread |
| `runBatch(start, end, batch_size)` | Independent batch over a contiguous range |
| `runBatchIntervals(intervals, batch_size)` | Batch over multiple inclusive ranges |
| `runRecurrentSequence(start, frame_count)` | Sequential recurrent inference |
| `cancel()` | Request cancellation of the current batch worker |
| `isRunning()` | Whether a background worker is active |

## Batch workers

Batch inference runs on dedicated `QThread` worker classes so AUTOMOC can
generate meta-object code from headers (no `.moc` include in `.cpp` files).

| Class | Header | Role |
|-------|--------|------|
| `BatchInferenceWorker` | `Inference/BatchInferenceWorker.hpp` | Single contiguous frame range |
| `MultiIntervalBatchWorker` | `Inference/MultiIntervalBatchWorker.hpp` | Multiple intervals with unified progress |

Both workers call `SlotAssembler::runBatchRangeOffline()`, push decoded
`FrameResult` entries into a shared `WriteReservation`, and emit
`progressChanged(current, total)` for connection to `InferenceController`.

### Single range

`runBatch(start, end, batch_size)` launches a `BatchInferenceWorker` on a
background `QThread`. Progress is reported via `batchProgressChanged(current, total)`.

### Multi-interval batch

`runBatchIntervals(intervals, batch_size)` accepts a vector of `(start, end)`
frame ranges (e.g. from a `DigitalIntervalSeries`) and processes each interval
sequentially on a single background thread. Progress is reported against the
**total** frame count across all intervals.

The `_onRunBatch()` dialog in `DeepLearningPropertiesWidget` offers two modes:

- **Manual range**: User specifies start/end frames directly.
- **From interval series**: User selects a `DigitalIntervalSeries` key from
  DataManager. The combo box auto-populates and refreshes via a DataManager
  observer callback cleaned up when the dialog is destroyed.

### Recurrent sequence

`runRecurrentSequence(start, frame_count)` runs frames in order so recurrent
memory bindings can feed prior outputs back into the model. Progress is reported
via `recurrentProgressChanged(current, total)`.

## Cancellation

All worker types share a `std::shared_ptr<std::atomic<bool>>` cancel flag owned
by the `InferenceController::Impl`. Calling `cancel()` sets this flag, which is
polled by `SlotAssembler::runBatchRangeOffline()` between batches. The same flag
is set in the destructor to ensure clean shutdown.

## Signals

| Signal | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `batchProgressChanged(current, total)` | Independent batch progress (queued from worker thread) |
| `recurrentProgressChanged(current, total)` | Recurrent sequence progress |
| `batchFinished(success, error_message)` | Batch or recurrent run completed |
| `runningChanged(running)` | Background worker started or stopped |

## Signal chain

```
Worker::progressChanged(current, total)
    → InferenceController::batchProgressChanged(current, total)
        → DeepLearningPropertiesWidget::batchProgressChanged(current, total)
            → DeepLearningViewWidget::updateRecurrentProgress(current, total)
```
